lines.en_lit: Rabbi Yoḥanan said: Great is repentance, as overrides a prohibition of the Torah. As it is stated “…Saying: If a man sends away his wife and she goes from him and becomes another man’s, may he return to her again? Will not that land be greatly polluted? But you have committed adultery with many lovers; and would you yet return to Me, said the Lord”

Rabbi Yonatan said: Great is repentance, which hastens the redemption, as it is stated: “And a redeemer will come to Zion, and to those who repent from transgression in Jacob” What is the reason a redeemer will come to Zion? because those who repent from transgression in Jacob.

Reish Lakish said: Great is repentance, as intentional sins are counted for him as unwitting transgressions, as it is stated: “Return, Israel, to the Lord your God, for you have stumbled in your iniquity” Doesn’t “iniquity” an intentional sin? Yet calls it stumbling, Is that so? Didn’t Reish Lakish say: Great is repentance, as one’s intentional sins are counted for him as merits, as it is stated: “And when the wicked turns from his wickedness, and does that which is lawful and right, he shall live thereby” not difficult: Here, out of love, there, out of fear,
Rabbi Shmuel bar Naḥmani said Rabbi Yonatan said: Great is repentance, which lengthens years of a person’s as it is stated: “When the wicked man turns from his wickedness he will preserve his life”

Rabbi Yitzḥak said: They say in the West, in the name of Rabba bar Mari: Come and see that the attribute of flesh and blood is unlike the attribute of the Holy One, Blessed be He. flesh and blood insults his friend with words, it is uncertain whether be appeased by him or not be appeased by him. And if you say he will be appeased, it is uncertain whether he be appeased by words or not be appeased by words

But the Holy One, Blessed be He, a person commits a transgression in private, is appeased by words, as it is stated: “Take with you words and return to God” And not only that, but considers it a favor as it is stated: “Accept that which is good” And not only that, but the verse ascribes him as though he had sacrificed bulls, as it is stated: “So we will render for bulls the offering of our lips” Lest you say obligatory bulls, the verse states: “I will heal their backsliding, I will love them freely”

It was taught Rabbi Meir would say: Great is repentance because the entire world is forgiven on account of individual who repents, as it is stated: “I will heal their backsliding, I will love them freely; for My anger has turned away from him” It does not say: From them, but “from him,”

What are the circumstances has repented? Rav Yehuda said: For example, the prohibited matter came to his hand a first time and a second time, and he was saved from it, Rav Yehuda demonstrated with the same woman at the same time the same place,

Rav Yehuda said Rav raised a contradiction: It is written: “Fortunate is he whose transgression is forgiven, whose sin is hidden” and it is written: “He who hides his transgressions shall not prosper” not difficult. Here a publicized sin; There, to a sin that is not publicized, Rav Zutra bar Toviya Rav Naḥman said: Here, to sins a person commits against another; There, to sins a person commits against God,

It was taught Rabbi Yosei bar Yehuda says: a person commits a transgression first time, he is forgiven; a second he is forgiven; a third he is forgiven; fourth he is not forgiven, as it is stated: “Thus said the Lord: For three transgressions of Israel, but for four I will not reverse it” And it says: “All these things does God do twice or three times with a man”

What is: And it says? Lest you say this applies to a community but not to an individual, come hear “All these things does God do twice or three times with a man,” From this onward, he is not forgiven, as it is stated: “For three transgressions of Israel, but for four I will not reverse it.”

The Sages taught transgressions that one confessed on this Yom Kippur, he should not confess them on another Yom Kippur, But if he repeated those he must confess on another Yom Kippur. And if he did not repeat them but did confess them again, about him the verse states: “As a dog that returns to its vomit, so is a fool who repeats his folly”

Rabbi Eliezer ben Ya’akov says: all the more so is he praiseworthy, as it is stated: “For I know my transgressions; and my sin is ever before me” But how do I establish the “Like a dog that returns to its vomit”? in accordance with the opinion of Rav Huna, as Rav Huna said: When a person commits a transgression and repeats it, it is permitted to him. Can it enter your mind that it is permitted to him Rather, say it becomes to him as if permitted.

one must detail the sin as it is stated: Please, this people have sinned a great sin, and have made themselves a god of gold” the statement of Rabbi Yehuda ben Bava. Rabbi Akiva says “Fortunate is he whose transgression is forgiven, whose sin is hidden” But what is which Moses said: “And have made themselves a god of gold” in accordance with of Rabbi Yannai, as Rabbi Yannai said: Moses said before the Holy One, Blessed be He: Master of the universe, silver and gold that you gave to the Jewish people in abundance, until they said: Enough, caused them to make a god of gold.

two good leaders arose for the Jewish people: Moses and David. Moses said: Let my disgrace be written, as it is stated: “Because you did not believe in Me, to sanctify Me” David said: Let my disgrace not be written, as it is stated: “Fortunate is he whose transgression is forgiven, whose sin is hidden”

A parable Moses and David to what this may be compared. to two women who were flogged in court One sinned and one ate unripe figs of the Sabbatical Year The woman who ate the unripe figs of the Sabbatical Year said to Please publicize for which flogged, so that will not say what that is being flogged for is what this is being flogged for. They brought unripe figs of the Sabbatical Year, and hung them around her neck, and announced before her, saying: She is receiving lashes on account of the Sabbatical Year.

One exposes the hypocrites due to desecration of name, as it is stated: “When a righteous man turns from his righteousness and commits iniquity, I will lay a stumbling block before him” The repentance of utterly people prevents suffering And although the sentence of judgment has been signed for suffering,

The tranquility of the wicked is ultimately their destruction, And authority buries one who owns it. He was naked when he entered and he will be naked when he leaves it, and if only his exit would be like his entrance, When Rav would leave to judge cases, he say this Of his own will, he goes to die, and he does not fulfill the will of his household and he goes empty-handed to his household, and if only his entrance would be like his exit,
When Rava would go to judge, he would say this
